mainEntity.name: Do temporary noise exposures qualify for comp in West Jordan construction?
mainEntity.text: Do temporary noise exposures qualify for comp in West Jordan construction?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if professionally tested exposure to harmful levels caused loss during that employment; Utah Code doesn't require chronic exposure, per 34A-2-503.

suggestedAnswer.text: Short-term high-noise projects (e.g., jackhammers) compensable if tests show hazard and loss follows. West Jordan sites often lack monitoring; workers file via symptoms + history. Stronger with co-worker affidavits. Avoid carrier 'de minimis' defenses legally.[4]

name: Comp for Short-Term Noise Exposure
description: Yes, if professionally tested exposure to harmful levels caused loss during that employment; Utah Code doesn't require chronic exposure, per 34A-2-503.
